text.title: methodologies Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Picking a stale slug from a historical row in this dispatcher without filesystem check.
antipattern.symptom: Downstream load fails with file-not-found; agent hallucinates content.
antipattern.root-cause: Dispatcher rows drift behind directory tree.
antipattern.fix: Always Glob the path; if not found, return empty candidate with escalation message.

antipattern.description: Treating this dispatcher as a manual ("the dispatcher said do X").
antipattern.symptom: Agent acts on the dispatcher's one-liner without reading the sibling AGENTS.md.
antipattern.root-cause: Skipped step in the routing-first principle.
antipattern.fix: After dispatch, always load the sibling AGENTS.md and act on its content.

antipattern.description: Multi-pick output: returning 2-3 candidate slugs from the dispatcher.
antipattern.symptom: Downstream gets confused; loads all three.
antipattern.root-cause: Confusing dispatcher (keyword to single slug) with catalogue (group to multi-pick).
antipattern.fix: Return exactly one candidate or null. For multi-pick, defer to [[files-reference]].
